ogTitle: Bon Bon vs Bookpurnong: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Bon Bon and Bookpurnong.
heading: Bon Bon vs Bookpurnong — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Bon Bon, South Australia and Bookpurnong, South Australia. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Bon Bon has a lower overall climate risk score (32/100) compared to Bookpurnong (35/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Bon Bon or Bookpurnong safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Bon Bon has a flood risk score of 32/100, while Bookpurnong scores 38/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Bon Bon scores 21/100 for bushfire risk. Bookpurnong scores 28/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Bon Bon or Bookpurnong?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Bon Bon has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 0.7% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
